perturbation.original_text: 2.2 BROOKS acknowledges that any failure by BROOKS to fulfill any obligation under this Agreement, or any breach by BROOKS of any provision herein, will constitute immediate and irreparable harm to CLIENT, which harm cannot be fully and/or adequately compensated in monetary damages and which will warrant injunctive relief, an order for specific performance, or any other available equitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 BROOKS acknowledges that any failure by BROOKS to fulfill any obligation under this Agreement, or any breach by BROOKS of any provision herein, will constitute harm to CLIENT, which harm can be compensated in monetary damages.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'immediate and irreparable harm to CLIENT, which harm cannot be fully and/or adequately compensated in monetary damages and which will warrant injunctive relief' to 'harm to CLIENT, which harm can be compensated in monetary damages' in section 2.2, this creates an in-text contradiction to the statement. This statement now implies monetary damages are sufficient, and equitable relief is not warranted, contradicting section 4, which discusses CLIENT seeking a protective order. This creates uncertainty about the CLIENT's recourse in case of compelled disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. COMPELLED DISCLOSURE. If BROOKS is legally compelled (whether by regulatory request, deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigation, demand or similar process) to disclose any Confidential Information, BROOKS shall immediately notify the CLIENT in writing of such requirements so that the CLIENT may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions hereof. BROOKS will use its best efforts, at Macintosh HD:Users:vibrant:Downloads:CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT.doc the CLIENT’S expense, to obtain or assist CLIENT in obtaining any such protective order. Failing the entry of a protective order to the receipt of a waiver hereunder, BROOKS may disclose, without liability hereunder, that portion (and only that portion) of Confidential Information that BROOKS has been advised by written opinion counsel reasonably acceptable to CLIENT that it is legally compelled to disclose; provided, however, that BROOKS agrees to use its best efforts to obtain assurance, at no cost to BROOKS, that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information by the person or persons to whom it is disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 2.2 states that failure to fulfill obligations would cause 'immediate and irreparable harm...which harm cannot be fully...compensated in monetary damages and which will warrant injunctive relief'. The changed text in section 2.2 states that failure to fulfill obligations would cause 'harm to CLIENT, which harm can be compensated in monetary damages'. This directly contradicts section 4, which allows the CLIENT to seek a protective order. The changed text implies that monetary damages are sufficient, negating the need for equitable relief, thus creating a direct conflict with section 4, making the two sections incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Under no circumstance will BROOKS approach any of CLIENT’s Private Label Condiment Customers for BROOKS’ own benefit.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 BROOKS will not intentionally approach any of CLIENT’s Private Label Condiment Customers for BROOKS’ own benefit.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "not intentionally" in section 2.3 weakens the clause that prohibits BROOKS to approach any of CLIENT’s Private Label Condiment Customers. Now BROOKS can claim they did not intentionally breach the contract. It creates an in-text contradiction with section 9, which states, 'This Agreement sets forth the entire and complete understanding of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior agreements, promises, covenants, arrangements, communications, representations or warranties, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof'. This now implies that BROOKS can claim that they approached without intent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Complete Agreement. This Agreement sets forth the entire and complete understanding of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior agreements, promises, covenants, arrangements, communications, representations or warranties, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof. No subsequent agreements or contracts between the Parties affect the obligations set forth herein unless this Agreement and the affected sections are explicitly cited in writing.
perturbation.justification: The addition of "not intentionally" in Section 2.3 weakens the original obligation, allowing BROOKS to potentially claim unintentional breaches. This contradicts Section 9 (Complete Agreement), which asserts the agreement sets forth the 'entire and complete understanding' and supersedes all prior communications. The 'not intentionally' clause introduces ambiguity and allows for claims outside the complete understanding, thus creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Exceptions Notwithstanding anything herein contained to the contrary, no obligation of confidentiality applies to any Confidential Information BROOKS: 3.1 Already possesses; 3.2 Develops independently; 3.3 Rightfully receives without obligation of confidentiality from a third party; 3.4 Inadvertently discloses where BROOKS has exercised reasonable care consistent with the effort BROOKS exercises with respect to the preservation of BROOKS’ own confidential information; 3.5 Discloses Confidential Information with the prior written consent of CLIENT ; 3.6 Discloses such Confidential Information to others which necessarily results from performing the services has contracted with BROOKS to perform. In addition, no obligation of confidentiality shall apply to any Confidential Information that is, or becomes, publicly available without breach of this Agreement or which is disclosed by the discloser to a third party without restriction.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Exceptions Notwithstanding anything herein contained to the contrary, no obligation of confidentiality applies to any Confidential Information BROOKS: 3.1 Already possesses; 3.2 Develops independently; 3.3 Rightfully receives without obligation of confidentiality from a third party; 3.4 Inadvertently discloses where BROOKS has exercised reasonable care; 3.5 Discloses Confidential Information with the prior written consent of CLIENT ; 3.6 Discloses such Confidential Information to others which necessarily results from performing the services has contracted with BROOKS to perform. In addition, no obligation of confidentiality shall apply to any Confidential Information that is, or becomes, publicly available without breach of this Agreement or which is disclosed by the discloser to a third party without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Inadvertently discloses where BROOKS has exercised reasonable care consistent with the effort BROOKS exercises with respect to the preservation of BROOKS’ own confidential information' to 'Inadvertently discloses where BROOKS has exercised reasonable care' in Section 3.4 creates an in-text contradiction. Section 3 now implies that BROOKS just has to use reasonable care and not 'consistent with the effort BROOKS exercises with respect to the preservation of BROOKS’ own confidential information', This means now BROOKS does not have to exercise reasonable care like it would with it's own confidential information, contradicting the spirit of the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Exceptions Notwithstanding anything herein contained to the contrary, no obligation of confidentiality applies to any Confidential Information BROOKS: 3.1 Already possesses; 3.2 Develops independently; 3.3 Rightfully receives without obligation of confidentiality from a third party; 3.4 Inadvertently discloses where BROOKS has exercised reasonable care; 3.5 Discloses Confidential Information with the prior written consent of CLIENT ; 3.6 Discloses such Confidential Information to others which necessarily results from performing the services has contracted with BROOKS to perform. In addition, no obligation of confidentiality shall apply to any Confidential Information that is, or becomes, publicly available without breach of this Agreement or which is disclosed by the discloser to a third party without restriction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that BROOKS must exercise 'reasonable care consistent with the effort BROOKS exercises with respect to the preservation of BROOKS’ own confidential information' when inadvertently disclosing confidential information. The changed text removes the phrase 'consistent with the effort BROOKS exercises with respect to the preservation of BROOKS’ own confidential information', implying a potentially lower standard of care. This creates an in-text contradiction as BROOKS is now held to a different standard than originally agreed upon regarding its own confidential information. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, as it now introduces a different standard of care than what was originally specified in the original text, leading to a clear conflict regarding the level of effort BROOKS must exert.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Material Upon termination of the business relationship between the Parties, BROOKS shall, upon written request of CLIENT, deliver any records, data, information and other documents, and all copies thereof, furnished by CLIENT to BROOKS. If CLIENT does not request in writing the return of the above material, BROOKS will retain said material for three (3) years after which BROOKS is free to dispose of material if it so desires and will do so after ten (10) days written notice to CLIENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Material Upon termination of the business relationship between the Parties, BROOKS shall, upon written request of CLIENT, deliver any records, data, information and other documents, and all copies thereof, furnished by CLIENT to BROOKS. If CLIENT does not request in writing the return of the above material, BROOKS will retain said material. BROOKS is free to dispose of material if it so desires and will do so after ten (10) days written notice to CLIENT.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'three (3) years after which' in Section 5, this creates an in-text contradiction regarding how long BROOKS must keep the information before disposing. This now gives BROOKS the power to dispose the information with only 10 days written notice, even if CLIENT never requested the data back. This creates uncertainty with what the true intent of the agreement is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return of Material Upon termination of the business relationship between the Parties, BROOKS shall, upon written request of CLIENT, deliver any records, data, information and other documents, and all copies thereof, furnished by CLIENT to BROOKS. If CLIENT does not request in writing the return of the above material, BROOKS will retain said material. BROOKS is free to dispose of material if it so desires and will do so after ten (10) days written notice to CLIENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that BROOKS would retain the material for three years before being free to dispose of it. The changed text removes the 'three (3) years after which' clause, allowing BROOKS to dispose of the material after only ten days' notice, regardless of whether CLIENT requested its return. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation, creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction within Section 5.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Survival. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until the earliest of: (a) one (1) year after the termination of the business relationship between the Parties; or (b) any alternate termination date specified in a written amendment modifying or waiving the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Survival. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until the termination of the business relationship between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(a) one (1) year after the termination of the business relationship between the Parties; or (b) any alternate termination date specified in a written amendment modifying or waiving the term of this Agreement.' in Section 7, now BROOKS is no longer held to the agreement after the end of the business relationship between the Parties. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5, which states BROOKS must either return the information upon request of CLIENT or retain the information. This can no longer be the case as Section 7 now states the agreement ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return of Material Upon termination of the business relationship between the Parties, BROOKS shall, upon written request of CLIENT, deliver any records, data, information and other documents, and all copies thereof, furnished by CLIENT to BROOKS. If CLIENT does not request in writing the return of the above material, BROOKS will retain said material. BROOKS is free to dispose of material if it so desires and will do so after ten (10) days written notice to CLIENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 7 allowed the agreement to remain in effect for one year after the termination of the business relationship or as specified in a written amendment. The changed text eliminates both of these conditions, stating the agreement terminates immediately upon the termination of the business relationship. This directly contradicts Section 5, which requires BROOKS to either return or retain CLIENT's information upon termination of the business relationship. As Section 7 now states the agreement ends, BROOKS is no longer bound to return or retain the information as specified in Section 5, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed in Section 7, creating a contradiction with the obligations in Section 5.
